heading: Tonnage Certificate
label: 14303 Tonnage Certificate
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised section 14303Source: International Convention on Tonnage Measurement of Ships.Section 14303(a) requires the Secretary to issue, on the request of the owner, an International Tonnage Certificate after measuring a vessel under this chapter. Section 14303(b) requires that the certificate be maintained as required by the Secretary.

Amendments2010—, struck out “International” before “Tonnage” and “(1969)” after “Certificate” in section catchline.Pub. L. 111–281, § 303(e)(1)(C)Subsec. (a). , inserted at end “For a vessel to which the Convention does not apply, the Secretary shall prescribe a certificate to be issued as evidence of a vessel’s measurement under this chapter.”Pub. L. 111–281, § 303(e)(1)(A)Subsec. (b). , inserted “issued under this section” after “The certificate”.Pub. L. 111–281, § 303(e)(1)(B)

(a). , inserted at end “For a vessel to which the Convention does not apply, the Secretary shall prescribe a certificate to be issued as evidence of a vessel’s measurement under this chapter.”Pub. children.label: a
children.text: After measuring a vessel under this chapter, the Secretary shall issue, on request of the owner, an International Tonnage Certificate (1969) and deliver it to the owner or master of the vessel. For a vessel to which the Convention does not apply, the Secretary shall prescribe a certificate to be issued as evidence of a vessel’s measurement under this chapter.

children.label: b
children.text: The certificate issued under this section shall be maintained as required by the Secretary.
